+"""Iterative repair loop - scan, fix, verify until clean."""
+
+from typing import Dict, List, Any, Tuple
+from pathlib import Path
+import asyncio
+
+from bcb.analyzer.llm_client import LLMClient
+from bcb.fixer.patch import PatchGenerator
+from bcb.fixer.verifier import PatchVerifier
+from bcb.scanner.patterns import PatternScanner
+from bcb.analyzer.security import SecurityAnalyzer
+
+
+class RepairLoop:
+    """Iterative repair loop."""
+
+    def __init__(
+        self,
+        llm_client: LLMClient,
+        codebase_info: Dict[str, Any],
+        architecture: Dict[str, Any],
+        max_iterations: int = 5,
+    ):
+        self.llm_client = llm_client
+        self.codebase_info = codebase_info
+        self.architecture = architecture
+        self.max_iterations = max_iterations
+        self.root_path = Path(codebase_info["root_path"])
+
+        self.patch_generator = PatchGenerator(llm_client, self.root_path)
+        self.verifier = PatchVerifier(self.root_path)
+
+    async def run(
+        self, root_causes: List[Dict[str, Any]], findings: List[Dict[str, Any]]
+    ) -> Tuple[List[Dict[str, Any]], int]:
+        """Run the repair loop."""
+        current_findings = findings
+        iteration = 0
+
+        # Sort root causes by priority
+        from bcb.analyzer.root_cause import RootCauseClusterer
+
+        clusterer = RootCauseClusterer(self.llm_client)
+        prioritized_causes = clusterer.get_high_priority_root_causes(root_causes)
+
+        for iteration in range(1, self.max_iterations + 1):
+            print(f"\n=== Repair Iteration {iteration} ===")
+
+            # Get critical and high severity root causes
+            critical_high = [
+                rc for rc in prioritized_causes if rc["severity"] in ["CRITICAL", "HIGH"]
+            ]
+
+            if not critical_high:
+                print("No critical/high severity issues remaining")
+                break
+
+            # Try to fix each root cause
+            fixed_count = 0
+            for root_cause in critical_high[:3]:  # Fix top 3 per iteration
+                print(f"Fixing: {root_cause['name']}")
+
+                success = await self._fix_root_cause(root_cause)
+                if success:
+                    fixed_count += 1
+                    # Remove findings associated with this root cause
+                    current_findings = [
+                        f for f in current_findings if f not in root_cause["findings"]
+                    ]
+
+            if fixed_count == 0:
+                print("No fixes applied successfully this iteration")
+                break
+
+            # Re-scan to check for new issues
+            print("Re-scanning codebase...")
+            scanner = PatternScanner(self.codebase_info, self.architecture)
+            new_findings = await scanner.scan()
+
+            # Verify new findings
+            analyzer = SecurityAnalyzer(self.llm_client)
+            verified_new = await analyzer.verify_findings(new_findings, self.codebase_info)
+
+            # Check if we introduced new critical/high issues
+            new_critical_high = [
+                f for f in verified_new if f["severity"] in ["CRITICAL", "HIGH"]
+            ]
+
+            if len(new_critical_high) > len(
+                [f for f in current_findings if f["severity"] in ["CRITICAL", "HIGH"]]
+            ):
+                print("Warning: New critical/high issues introduced")
+
+            current_findings = verified_new
+
+            # Check if we're done
+            remaining_critical_high = [
+                f for f in current_findings if f["severity"] in ["CRITICAL", "HIGH"]
+            ]
+            if not remaining_critical_high:
+                print("All critical/high issues resolved!")
+                break
+
+        return current_findings, iteration
+
+    async def _fix_root_cause(self, root_cause: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
+        """Attempt to fix a single root cause."""
+        max_attempts = 3
+
+        for attempt in range(1, max_attempts + 1):
+            print(f"  Attempt {attempt}/{max_attempts}")
+
+            # Generate patch
+            patch_data = await self.patch_generator.generate_patch(root_cause)
+
+            if "error" in patch_data:
+                print(f"  Patch generation failed: {patch_data['error']}")
+                continue
+
+            # Review patch with LLM
+            files_content = {}
+            for file_path in root_cause.get("affected_files", [])[:5]:
+                abs_path = self.root_path / file_path
+                try:
+                    with open(abs_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
+                        files_content[file_path] = f.read()
+                except Exception:
+                    pass
+
+            review = await self.llm_client.review_patch(patch_data, files_content)
+
+            if not review.get("approved", False) and review.get("confidence", 0) > 0.7:
+                print(f"  Patch rejected by LLM: {review.get('issues', [])}")
+                continue
+
+            # Create backup
+            affected_files = [p["file"] for p in patch_data.get("patches", [])]
+            backup_msg = await self.patch_generator.create_backup(affected_files)
+            print(f"  {backup_msg}")
+
+            # Apply patch
+            apply_result = await self.patch_generator.apply_patch(patch_data)
+
+            if not apply_result["success"]:
+                print(f"  Patch application failed: {apply_result['errors']}")
+                # Restore backup
+                await self.patch_generator.restore_backup()
+                continue
+
+            # Verify patch
+            verify_result = await self.verifier.verify_patch(
+                apply_result, apply_result["applied_patches"]
+            )
+
+            if not verify_result["success"]:
+                print(f"  Verification failed: {verify_result['errors']}")
+                # Restore backup
+                await self.patch_generator.restore_backup()
+                continue
+
+            # Success!
+            print(f"  ✓ Fixed successfully")
+            return True
+
+        print(f"  ✗ Failed after {max_attempts} attempts")
+        return False
+

+"""Report generation in multiple formats."""
+
+import json

+
+class ReportGenerator:

+        findings: List[Dict[str, Any]],
+        root_causes: List[Dict[str, Any]],
+        iteration: int = 0,
+        final: bool = False,
+    ) -> Path:
+        """Generate a report."""
+        # Generate both markdown and JSON
+        timestamp = datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
+
+        # Prepare report data
+        report_data = self._prepare_report_data(
+            codebase_info, architecture, findings, root_causes, iteration, timestamp
+        )
+

+
+        # Generate JSON report
+        json_path = await self._generate_json(report_data, final)
+
+        return md_path
+

+        iteration: int,

+        severity_counts = {"CRITICAL": 0, "HIGH": 0, "MEDIUM": 0, "LOW": 0}

+        t = Template(template)

+"""CVSS-lite severity scoring."""
+
+from typing import Dict, Any
+
+
+class SeverityScorer:
+    """Calculate severity scores for findings."""
+
+    # Base severity scores

+        "CRITICAL": 9.0,
+        "HIGH": 7.0,
+        "MEDIUM": 5.0,
+        "LOW": 3.0,
+    }
+
+    # CWE risk multipliers

+        "CWE-89": 1.2,  # SQL Injection
+        "CWE-78": 1.2,  # Command Injection
+        "CWE-79": 1.1,  # XSS
+        "CWE-798": 1.3,  # Hardcoded Credentials
+        "CWE-306": 1.2,  # Missing Authentication
+        "CWE-639": 1.1,  # IDOR
+        "CWE-352": 1.0,  # CSRF
+        "CWE-918": 1.1,  # SSRF
+    }
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def calculate_score(finding: Dict[str, Any]) -> float:
+        """Calculate a CVSS-lite score for a finding."""
+        base_score = SeverityScorer.SEVERITY_SCORES.get(finding.get("severity", "LOW"), 3.0)
+
+        # Apply CWE multiplier
+        cwe = finding.get("cwe", "")
+        multiplier = SeverityScorer.CWE_MULTIPLIERS.get(cwe, 1.0)
+
+        # Apply confidence factor
+        confidence = finding.get("confidence", 0.8)
+
+        # Apply blast radius factor
+        blast_radius = finding.get("blast_radius", "unknown")
+        blast_multiplier = {
+            "critical": 1.2,
+            "high": 1.1,
+            "medium": 1.0,
+            "low": 0.9,
+            "unknown": 1.0,
+        }.get(blast_radius, 1.0)
+
+        score = base_score * multiplier * confidence * blast_multiplier
+
+        # Cap at 10.0
+        return min(score, 10.0)
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def get_severity_from_score(score: float) -> str:
+        """Get severity level from score."""
+        if score >= 9.0:
+            return "CRITICAL"
+        elif score >= 7.0:
+            return "HIGH"
+        elif score >= 4.0:
+            return "MEDIUM"
+        else:
+            return "LOW"
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def prioritize_findings(findings: list[Dict[str, Any]]) -> list[Dict[str, Any]]:
+        """Sort findings by priority (score)."""
+        scored = []
+        for finding in findings:
+            score = SeverityScorer.calculate_score(finding)
+            finding["score"] = score
+            scored.append(finding)
+
+        return sorted(scored, key=lambda x: x["score"], reverse=True)

+# BCB Setup and Usage Guide
+
+## Installation
+
+1. Install dependencies:
+```bash
+poetry install
+```
+
+Or with pip:
+```bash
+pip install typer pyyaml tree-sitter tree-sitter-python tree-sitter-javascript tree-sitter-typescript aiohttp gitpython rich pydantic jinja2
+```
+
+2. Set up IBM Bob API (optional - for LLM features):
+```bash
+export IBM_BOB_API_KEY="your-api-key"
+export IBM_BOB_API_URL="http://localhost:8080/v1/chat/completions"
+export IBM_BOB_MODEL="gpt-4"
+```
+
+## Quick Start
+
+### Scan a codebase (report only):
+```bash
+python -m bcb.cli scan ./test_project --no-fix
+```
+
+### Scan and auto-fix:
+```bash
+python -m bcb.cli scan ./test_project
+```
+
+### Filter by severity:
+```bash
+python -m bcb.cli scan ./test_project --severity critical,high
+```
+
+### Fix with custom iteration limit:
+```bash
+python -m bcb.cli fix ./test_project --max-iterations 3
+```
+
+## Test with Sample Vulnerable App
+
+A sample vulnerable Flask application is included in `test_project/`:
+
+```bash
+# Scan the test project
+python -m bcb.cli scan ./test_project --no-fix
+
+# This will detect:
+# - Hardcoded secrets (API keys, passwords)
+# - SQL injection vulnerabilities
+# - Command injection
+# - Path traversal
+# - Missing authentication
+# - Weak password hashing
+# - Mass assignment
+# - Debug mode enabled
+# - .env file not in .gitignore
+```
+
+## Expected Output
+
+BCB will:
+1. Discover the codebase structure
+2. Map the architecture
+3. Scan for vulnerability patterns
+4. Verify findings with LLM (if configured)
+5. Cluster findings by root cause
+6. Generate a detailed report (`bcb-report.md`)
+7. Optionally apply fixes and verify
+
+## Report Location
+
+Reports are generated in the scanned directory:
+- `bcb-report-iter0.md` - Initial scan report
+- `bcb-report-final.md` - Final report after fixes
+- `bcb-report-final.json` - JSON format report
+
+## Architecture
+
+```
+bcb/
+├── cli.py              # CLI entry point
+├── scanner/            # Codebase scanning
+│   ├── codebase.py     # File discovery
+│   ├── architecture.py # Architecture mapping
+│   └── patterns.py     # Pattern matching
+├── analyzer/           # LLM-powered analysis
+│   ├── llm_client.py   # IBM Bob client
+│   ├── security.py     # Security verification
+│   └── root_cause.py   # Root cause clustering
+├── fixer/              # Automated patching
+│   ├── patch.py        # Patch generation/application
+│   ├── verifier.py     # Patch verification
+│   └── repair_loop.py  # Iterative repair
+└── reporter/           # Report generation
+    ├── report.py       # Markdown/JSON reports
+    └── severity.py     # CVSS-lite scoring
+```
+
+## Vulnerability Patterns
+
+BCB detects 40+ vulnerability patterns including:
+
+**Critical:**
+- Hardcoded secrets and credentials
+- SQL/NoSQL/Command injection
+- Authentication bypasses
+- IDOR vulnerabilities
+
+**High:**
+- CORS misconfigurations
+- Missing CSRF protection
+- SSRF vulnerabilities
+- Unrestricted file uploads
+
+**Medium:**
+- Information disclosure
+- Missing rate limiting
+- Logic bugs in state management
+- Async/concurrency issues
+
+**Low:**
+- Architectural smells
+- Code quality issues
+
+## Configuration
+
+Patterns are defined in `config/patterns.yaml`. You can:
+- Add new patterns
+- Modify severity levels
+- Customize fix templates
+- Adjust LLM prompts
+
+## Limitations
+
+- LLM features require IBM Bob API access
+- Some fixes may require manual review
+- Complex architectural changes need human oversight
+- Test coverage affects verification quality
+
+## Troubleshooting
+
+**No LLM verification:**
+- Set IBM_BOB_API_KEY environment variable
+- Or run with `--no-fix` for pattern-only scanning
+
+**Patch application fails:**
+- Ensure git is installed
+- Check file permissions
+- Review patch diffs manually
+
+**Tests fail after patching:**
+- Review the verification output
+- Check `bcb-report.md` for details
+- Patches are automatically reverted on failure

+"""Demo script showing BCB usage."""
+
+import asyncio
+from pathlib import Path
+import sys
+
+# Add parent directory to path
+sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent))
+
+from bcb.scanner.codebase import CodebaseScanner
+from bcb.scanner.architecture import ArchitectureMapper
+from bcb.scanner.patterns import PatternScanner
+from bcb.analyzer.llm_client import LLMClient
+from bcb.analyzer.security import SecurityAnalyzer
+from bcb.analyzer.root_cause import RootCauseClusterer
+from bcb.reporter.report import ReportGenerator
+
+
+async def demo_scan():
+    """Demo: Scan the test project."""
+    print("=" * 60)
+    print("BCB Demo: Scanning test_project")
+    print("=" * 60)
+
+    # Path to test project
+    test_path = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "test_project"
+
+    # Phase 1: Discovery
+    print("\n[1/6] Discovering codebase...")
+    scanner = CodebaseScanner(test_path)
+    codebase_info = await scanner.scan()
+    print(f"  ✓ Found {codebase_info['file_count']} files")
+    print(f"  ✓ Languages: {', '.join(codebase_info['languages'])}")
+    print(f"  ✓ Frameworks: {', '.join(codebase_info['frameworks'])}")
+
+    # Phase 2: Architecture mapping
+    print("\n[2/6] Mapping architecture...")
+    arch_mapper = ArchitectureMapper(codebase_info)
+    architecture = await arch_mapper.map()
+    print(f"  ✓ Modules: {len(architecture['modules'])}")
+    print(f"  ✓ Trust boundaries: {len(architecture['trust_boundaries'])}")
+
+    # Phase 3: Pattern scanning
+    print("\n[3/6] Scanning for vulnerabilities...")
+    pattern_scanner = PatternScanner(codebase_info, architecture)
+    findings = await pattern_scanner.scan()
+    print(f"  ✓ Found {len(findings)} potential issues")
+
+    # Show severity breakdown
+    severity_counts = {}
+    for finding in findings:
+        sev = finding["severity"]
+        severity_counts[sev] = severity_counts.get(sev, 0) + 1
+
+    print("\n  Severity breakdown:")
+    for sev in ["CRITICAL", "HIGH", "MEDIUM", "LOW"]:
+        count = severity_counts.get(sev, 0)
+        if count > 0:
+            print(f"    {sev}: {count}")
+
+    # Phase 4: Show sample findings
+    print("\n[4/6] Sample findings:")
+    critical = [f for f in findings if f["severity"] == "CRITICAL"][:3]
+    for i, finding in enumerate(critical, 1):
+        print(f"\n  {i}. {finding['name']}")
+        print(f"     File: {finding['file']}:{finding['line']}")
+        print(f"     Code: {finding['matched_code'][:60]}...")
+
+    # Phase 5: Root cause clustering (without LLM for demo)
+    print("\n[5/6] Clustering root causes...")
+    # Simple clustering by pattern ID
+    from collections import defaultdict
+
+    pattern_groups = defaultdict(list)
+    for finding in findings:
+        pattern_groups[finding["id"]].append(finding)
+
+    root_causes = []
+    for pattern_id, group in pattern_groups.items():
+        if group:
+            root_causes.append(
+                {
+                    "id": f"RC-{pattern_id}",
+                    "pattern_id": pattern_id,
+                    "name": group[0]["name"],
+                    "description": group[0]["description"],
+                    "severity": group[0]["severity"],
+                    "findings": group,
+                    "affected_files": list(set(f["file"] for f in group)),
+                    "fix_strategy": group[0].get("fix_template", ""),
+                    "confidence": 0.8,
+                }
+            )
+
+    print(f"  ✓ Identified {len(root_causes)} root causes")
+
+    # Phase 6: Generate report
+    print("\n[6/6] Generating report...")
+    report_gen = ReportGenerator(test_path)
+    report_path = await report_gen.generate(
+        codebase_info=codebase_info,
+        architecture=architecture,
+        findings=findings,
+        root_causes=root_causes,
+        iteration=0,
+    )
+    print(f"  ✓ Report saved to: {report_path}")
+
+    # Summary
+    print("\n" + "=" * 60)
+    print("SUMMARY")
+    print("=" * 60)
+    print(f"Total findings: {len(findings)}")
+    print(f"Root causes: {len(root_causes)}")
+    print(f"Critical: {severity_counts.get('CRITICAL', 0)}")
+    print(f"High: {severity_counts.get('HIGH', 0)}")
+    print(f"Medium: {severity_counts.get('MEDIUM', 0)}")
+    print(f"Low: {severity_counts.get('LOW', 0)}")
+
+    if severity_counts.get("CRITICAL", 0) > 0:
+        print("\n⚠️  CRITICAL vulnerabilities found - immediate action required!")
+    elif severity_counts.get("HIGH", 0) > 0:
+        print("\n⚠️  HIGH severity issues found - fix before production")
+    else:
+        print("\n✓ No critical or high severity issues")
+
+    print(f"\nView full report: {report_path}")
+    print("=" * 60)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    asyncio.run(demo_scan())
+
+# Made with Bob
